latin: Cor enim ambitiosi numquam quiescit; sed vel honorem, quem non habet appetit, vel eum quem habet amittere metuit.
english: For the heart of the ambitious never rests: either it craves the honor it does not have, or it dreads to lose the one it has.
from: The Lord Who Fled the Crown

latin: Sicut enim, qui intoxicaret fontem, causa esset mortis omnium potantium et morientium de eo; sic est et de scandalo.
english: For just as one who would poison a fountain would be the cause of death to all who drink of it and die thereof, so it is also with scandal.
from: The Millstone and the Little Ones

latin: Oves meæ vocem meam audiunt, credendo corde, et obediendo opere; et ego cognosco eas.
english: My sheep hear my voice, believing in heart and obeying in work; and I know them.

latin: Beati qui lugent, ecce vigilia, quoniam ipsi consolabuntur, ecce festum.
english: Blessed are they that mourn — behold the vigil; for they shall be comforted — behold the feast.

latin: Sicut agnus coram tondente se obmutescet, et non aperiet os suum.
english: Like a lamb before its shearer, He shall be dumb, and shall not open His mouth.

latin: Horrenda prorsus impietas, quæ tantis injuriis satiari non potuit, quin potius ferali rabie fremens, impio judici, tamquam rabido cani, animam justi deglutiendam exposuit.
english: Horrible impiety indeed, which could not be sated with so many injuries, but rather, raging with savage madness, exposed the soul of the just One to the wicked judge, as to a mad dog, to be devoured.

latin: Da mihi misero te desiderare, desiderando quærere, quærendo invenire, inveniendo amare, amando mala mea redimere, redempta non iterare.
english: Grant to me, a wretch, to desire you; by desiring, to seek you; by seeking, to find you; by finding, to love you; by loving, to redeem my evils; and, once redeemed, to do them no more.

latin: Non quantum des, sed ex quanto, attendit Deus.
english: God regards not how much you give, but out of how much you give it.

latin: Magna quippe licentia, et magna gratia est mittere manum in latus illud dulcissimum, in latus illud saluberrimum, in latus, inquam, unde ab ira salvamur, unde a culpa sanamur, unde in gratia regeneramur, unde in gloria sublimamur.
english: Great indeed is the liberty, and great the grace, to put one's hand into that sweetest side, that most healing side — into the side, I say, whence we are saved from wrath, whence we are healed of our guilt, whence we are reborn into grace, whence we are lifted up into glory.

latin: Dilexit, et diligit sic vehementer et non diligitur, cum tamen ad talem et tantum ignem non solum calefieri, sed etiam ardere deberemus.
english: He loved, and he loves, so vehemently, and yet is not loved in return — though at such and so great a fire we ought not only to be warmed, but even to burn.

latin: Magna securitas est nihil timere praeter Deum, et stultus timor est, quo plus timetur humana indignatio, quam divina.
english: It is a great security to fear nothing but God; and foolish is the fear by which human anger is dreaded more than the divine.
from: Fear Not Those Who Kill the Body

latin: Quos autem non potes corripiendo, aut monendo vel accusando convertere, studeas saltem orando acquirere.
english: But those whom you cannot convert by correcting, or by warning, or by accusing, strive at least to win by praying.

latin: Tristis est anima mea usque ad mortem.
english: My soul is sorrowful unto death.

latin: Ignem veni mittere in terram, et quid volo, nisi ut accendatur?
english: I came to cast fire upon the earth, and what do I will, but that it be kindled?

latin: Et ideo, aliis luminaribus exstinctis, una candela in officio tenebrarum servatur accensa, quia lumen fidei in sola remansit virgine Maria.
english: And therefore, when the other lights are extinguished, one candle is kept burning in the office of Tenebrae, because the light of faith remained in the Virgin Mary alone.

latin: Videtis me, agnoscite me; videtis facta, agnoscite factorem.
english: You see me — then know me; you see the deeds — then know the doer.

latin: Magis enim voluit infirmitatem suam humiliter recognoscendo inter membra Christi numerari, quam ejus nomen immerito usurpando, ab ipso Christi corpore abscindi.
english: For he chose rather, by humbly acknowledging his own weakness, to be numbered among the members of Christ, than, by usurping the name that was not his, to be cut off from the very body of Christ.

latin: Ecce innocens inter peccatores, justus inter reprobos, pius inter impios; in quo nullum peccatum potuit inveniri.
english: Behold the innocent one among sinners, the just one among the reprobate, the holy one among the impious; in whom no sin could be found.

latin: Ipsa autem lux, de qua testimonium perhibebat, erat ab æterno lux vera sine falsitate, sine umbra, et sine participatione, sed per essentiam.
english: But that Light, of which he bore witness, was from eternity the true Light: without falsehood, without shadow, without participation, but Light by its very essence.

latin: Possibilius enim credebat virginem sine viro posse concipere, quam Mariam posse peccare.
english: For he held it more possible that a virgin could conceive without man, than that Mary could sin.

latin: Magis enim operando quis proficit quam loquendo, et plus movent exempla quam verba, quia major est splendor operis quam sermonis.
english: For one profits more by doing than by speaking, and examples move more than words, because the splendor of a deed is greater than that of speech.
from: Salt of the Earth, Light of the World

latin: Prius ergo præcinge lumbos, et tunc accende lucernam; prius declina a malo, et tunc fac bonum; prius quiesce agere perverse, et tunc disce benefacere.
english: First, then, gird your loins, and then light the lamp; first turn away from evil, and then do good; first cease to act perversely, and then learn to do well.

latin: Intenta erat Martha quomodo pasceret Dominum; intenta erat Maria quomodo pasceretur a Domino.
english: Martha was intent on how she might feed the Lord; Mary was intent on how she might be fed by the Lord.

latin: Et significat quod ubi Christus cœnaturis est, lagena aquæ lacrymarum in expiatione debet præcedere.
english: And this signifies that where Christ is to sup, the jar of the water of tears must go before, in expiation.

latin: Oratio enim viri justi cœlos penetrat; quomodo ergo in terris hostes non vincat?
english: For the prayer of a just man pierces the heavens; how then shall it not, on earth, conquer his enemies?

latin: Una ergo solummodo carnalis est causa, id est fornicatio, una spiritualis, hoc est timor Dei, ut uxor dimittatur; nulla autem causa est ut, vivente ea, quæ relicta est, alia ducatur.
english: There is therefore one carnal cause only, that is fornication, and one spiritual, that is the fear of God, for which a wife may be put away; but there is no cause at all that, while she who is forsaken yet lives, another should be taken.

latin: Hostia enim Deo placabilis est salus hominum.
english: For the sacrifice pleasing to God is the salvation of men.

latin: Necessarium enim erit, ut aliquando ita cogites te praesentem cogitatione tua, ac si tunc temporis ibi praesens fuisses quando passus fuit.
english: For it will be needful that you sometimes so reckon yourself present in your thought, as though you had then and there been present when He suffered.

latin: Dum enim vis vindicare vestem corporis, pretiosissimum animæ vestimentum perdis.
english: For while you long to avenge the garment of your body, you lose the most precious garment of your soul.

latin: Mirabilis venator est Dominus, qui per agnos, sive oves, capit et devincit lupos; per patientiam vincit potentiam.
english: A wondrous hunter is the Lord, who by lambs, by sheep, captures and conquers the wolves; by patience He overcomes power.

latin: Bene in igne Spiritus apparuit, quia ab omni corde quod replet, torporem frigoris excutit, et hoc in desiderio suæ æternitatis accendit.
english: Well did the Spirit appear in fire, for from every heart that He fills He shakes out the torpor of cold, and kindles it in the desire of His own eternity.
from: The Fire That Does Not Consume

latin: Fides ardebat; sed humana infirmitas in profundum trahebat.
english: His faith was burning; but human weakness was dragging him down into the deep.
from: On the Waves of the Night

latin: Plus itaque mihi contulit Deus redimendo, quam creando: creando quippe me mihi dedit, redimendo autem et seipsum dedit, et me mihi reddidit.
english: God gave me more in redeeming than in creating: for in creating He gave me to myself, but in redeeming He gave both Himself, and gave me back to myself.

latin: Ille languens, istæ tristes, omnes dilecti.
english: He was the one languishing, these two were grieving, and all of them were beloved.

latin: In facie ejus quidam fulgor divinæ gratiæ relucebat, qui animos intuentium attrahebat.
english: In His face a certain radiance of divine grace shone forth, which drew to itself the souls of those who beheld Him.

latin: Verae revelationes, et sentimenta spiritualia secretorum Dei, non veniunt per desiderium, neque per aliquod conamen vel studium quod anima habeat in se; sed tantum veniunt per puram bonitatem Dei in animam, quae est in magna humilitate, et in magno timore, ac reverentia Dei.
english: True revelations, and spiritual feelings of the secrets of God, do not come through desire, nor through any striving or study the soul has in itself; but they come only through the pure goodness of God into a soul that dwells in great humility, and in great fear, and reverence of God.

latin: Domina igitur orante et lacrymas dulciter emittente, ecce subito venit Dominus Jesus in albissimis vestibus gloriae et Resurrectionis novitatis suae; vultu sereno, speciosus, gloriosus, et gaudens, ac Matri desolatae et moerenti totus festivus apparens.
english: And so, as our Lady was praying and sweetly pouring out her tears, behold, suddenly the Lord Jesus came in garments most white, of the glory and newness of His Resurrection; with serene face, beautiful, glorious, and rejoicing, and to His desolate and mourning Mother He appeared, all festal.
from: The First to See Him Risen

latin: Mulier, quæ fuerat janua mortis, prima prædicat resurrectionem, et ostendit januam vitæ.
english: The woman who had been the gate of death is the first to preach the Resurrection, and shows the gate of life.

latin: Conveniens enim fuit, ut verus flos, id est Christus, conciperetur in flore, id est, in Nazareth; et de flore, id est, de Beata Virgine; et cum floribus, id est, tempore florum.
english: For it was fitting that the true Flower, that is Christ, should be conceived in the flower, that is, in Nazareth; and of the flower, that is, of the Blessed Virgin; and with the flowers, that is, in the season of flowers.
from: The Word Made Flesh in Her

latin: Passurus Dominus pro omni mundo, et universas nationes suo sanguine redempturus, venit et moratur in Bethania, id est in domo obedientiæ.
english: The Lord, about to suffer for the whole world, and to redeem all nations by his blood, comes and abides in Bethany, that is, in the house of obedience.

latin: O unicum bonum meum! O singulare gaudium meum! Quid faciam cum te sic videam?
english: O my one good! O my one and only joy! What shall I do, when I see You like this?

latin: Et ideo, quam saepe locorum recreabatur visitationibus, et quem genuerat fideli spiritu, mentis complexabatur amplexibus!
english: And so, how often was she refreshed by visiting those places, and embraced in the arms of her mind, with faithful spirit, the One she had borne!

latin: Factus est servus, ut nos reges faceret; factus est advena et peregrinus, ut nos ad patriam et regnum suum perduceret.
english: He became a servant, that he might make us kings; he became a stranger and a pilgrim, that he might lead us to his homeland and his kingdom.
from: The Lord Goes Down to Jordan

latin: Quid enim stercore sordidius? Et tamen si eo bene utaris, quid fructuosius?
english: For what is filthier than dung? And yet, if you use it well, what is more fruitful?

latin: Tunc vere possidebis terram, quando inhæseris ei, qui fecit cœlum et terram.
english: Then truly shall you possess the earth, when you cleave to Him who made heaven and earth.

latin: Praemissus est Joannes, velut vox ante Verbum, aurora ante solem, praeco ante Judicem, servus ante Dominum, amicus ante Sponsum.
english: John was sent ahead, as the voice before the Word, the dawn before the sun, the herald before the Judge, the servant before the Lord, the friend before the Bridegroom.
from: The Light Goes Up Into Galilee

latin: Convivante itaque Herode, paulo ante horam diei nonam, sanctus Joannes Baptista est decollatus in custodia, clamando voce magna: Domine Deus meus, tibi commendo spiritum meum.
english: And so, while Herod feasted, a little before the ninth hour, holy John the Baptist was beheaded in his cell, crying out with a loud voice: My Lord and my God, into your hands I commend my spirit.

latin: Infelix mercator Judas, qui tantum dedit pro tanto, Deum pro nummo, verum pro vano, æternum pro transitorio!
english: Wretched merchant Judas, who gave so much for so little: God for a coin, the true for the empty, the eternal for the passing!

latin: Non clamor, sed amor sonat in aure Dei.
english: It is not the cry, but the love, that sounds in the ear of God.

latin: Abundat dæmonis malitia; superabundat divina clementia.
english: The malice of the demon abounds; the divine mercy abounds beyond it.

latin: Quisquis ergo cæcitatis suæ tenebras agnoscit, quisquis hoc quod sibi deest lumen æternitatis intelligit, clamet medullis cordis, clamet et vocibus oris, dicens: Jesu, fili David, miserere mei.
english: Whoever therefore acknowledges the darkness of his own blindness, whoever understands that the light of eternity is wanting to him, let him cry out from the marrow of his heart, let him cry out also with the voice of his lips, saying: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.

latin: Sequere et tu pretiosissimum cœli terræque thesaurum; et porta vel pedes vel manus, brachiaque sustenta; terræ defluentes minutatim pretiosissimi sanguinis guttas curiosius collige; et pulverem pedum ejus linge.
english: Follow, you too, the most precious treasure of heaven and earth; carry either His feet or His hands, and bear up His arms; gather up with care the drops of most precious blood that fall little by little upon the ground; and kiss the dust of His feet.

latin: Vera siquidem pænitentia est, sine intermissione temporis, de præteritis malis dolere, et sic plangere commissa, ut non committat plangenda.
english: For true penance is, without any pause of time, to grieve for past evils, and so to bewail what is done that one does not do the things to be bewailed.

latin: Abneget semetipsum per propriæ voluntatis renuntiationem; non tantum sua vel suos, sed suum intellectum, suum affectum, suum sensum.
english: Let him deny himself by the renunciation of his own will: not only his things or his kin, but his own understanding, his own affection, his own sense.

latin: Et vidit ibi, non tam corporis, quam mentis et miserantis intuitu, hominem.
english: And he saw there a man — not so much with the gaze of the body as with the gaze of the mind, and of one who has pity.

latin: Modicus sermo, sed immensum benignitatis continet pelagus; ubi enim misericordia postulatur, ibi universa comprehensa sunt bona.
english: A short word, but it holds within it a measureless sea of kindness; for where mercy is asked, there all good things are contained together.

latin: Domine Jesu Christe, non sum dignus ut intres sub tectum carnis meæ, quia puer sensualitatis jacet paralyticus, dissolutus morbo peccati in domo corporis mei.
english: Lord Jesus Christ, I am not worthy that you should enter under the roof of my flesh, for the boy of my sensuality lies paralysed, undone by the sickness of sin in the house of my body.

latin: Ó mira benignitas Christi in discipulum durum, Domini in servum nequissimum!
english: O wondrous kindness of Christ toward a hardened disciple, of the Lord toward the most wicked of servants!

latin: Et erat pernoctans in oratione Dei, non seculi; non pro se tamquam infirmus et indigens, sed pro nobis tamquam pius et clemens.
english: And He kept watch all night long in the prayer of God, not of the world; not for Himself as one weak and in need, but for us, as one tender and merciful.

latin: Nomen quod, secundum Bernardum, est mel in ore, in aure melos, in corde jubilus.
english: A name which, according to Bernard, is honey in the mouth, a melody in the ear, a jubilee in the heart.

latin: Zelus domus tuæ comedit me.
english: The zeal of thy house hath eaten me up.

latin: Ut nudus nudum Christum sequatur.
english: That, naked, he may follow the naked Christ.

latin: Crux apparebit sole luculentior existens, ut non sit necessitas accusationis cum viderint crucem.
english: The Cross shall appear, shining brighter than the sun, so that no accusation will be needed once they have seen the Cross.
from: The Sign of the Cross in Heaven

latin: Joannes enim interpretatur, in quo est gratia; ipse vero Christum manifestavit, per quem gratia et veritas facta est.
english: For John is interpreted 'in whom is grace'; and he himself made Christ manifest, through whom grace and truth came to be.

latin: Gira et regira, versa et reversa, et non invenies pacem et requiem, nisi in solo Jesu.
english: Turn and turn again, search and search once more, and you will find no peace and no rest, except in Jesus alone.

latin: Sic doctor Evangelicus debet primo errores destruere, ut melius possit veritatem adstruere.
english: So must the teacher of the Gospel first destroy errors, that he may the better build up the truth.

latin: Levate per spem capita vestra, id est mentes vestras a terrenis ad cælestia exspectatione gaudiosa erigite.
english: Lift up your heads by hope — that is, raise your minds from earthly things to heavenly with a joyful expectation.

latin: Vulpes foveas habent, ad quiescendum et latitandum, et volucres cœli nidos habent ad quos ascendunt et confugiunt, Filius autem hominis non habet ubi caput suum reclinet.
english: The foxes have holes, to rest and to hide in, and the birds of the air have nests to which they ascend and flee; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
from: No Place to Lay His Head

latin: Si vis ergo fructus justitiæ ad finem tenere, recede de via, et plantare in loco secreto, ut nec mundus tecum aliquid habeat commune, nec tu cum mundo.
english: If, then, you would hold the fruit of justice to the end, withdraw from the road, and be planted in a hidden place, that the world may have nothing in common with you, nor you with the world.

latin: Noe, etsi tacebat voce, opere loquebatur; si et lingua silebat, fabricatione clamabat.
english: Noah, though he was silent with his voice, spoke by his work; though his tongue was still, by the building he cried out.
from: The Day That Comes Without Warning

latin: Sanatur quem tangit Jesus, sanatur quem tangit Salvator, quia ipse est salus et vita; non aliunde quærit herbas, ipse est et medicus, et medicina.
english: Whom Jesus touches is healed, whom the Savior touches is healed, for he himself is salvation and life; he seeks no herbs from elsewhere — he himself is both the physician and the medicine.

latin: Praesentia Salvatoris, tormentum est daemonum.
english: The presence of the Savior is the torment of the demons.

latin: Stabat Maria levans brachia in altum; et dum tangere eum valuit, caput et manus ejus dependentia trahebat supra triste pectus suum.
english: Mary stood, lifting her arms on high; and when she could reach him, she drew his hanging head and hands down upon her sorrowing breast.

latin: Nos ergo, exemplo Christi et Joannis, agitemur a Spiritu Sancto, non per hypocrisim a maligno, in desertum loci et solitudinis, vel saltem in desertum nostri pectoris et contemplationis, ut quieti a strepitu mundi, melius Deo vacemus.
english: Let us then, after the example of Christ and of John, be driven by the Holy Spirit — and not, through hypocrisy, by the evil one — into the desert of place and solitude, or at the least into the desert of our own breast and of contemplation, that, resting from the noise of the world, we may better wait upon God.

latin: Quis ita dormit quando voluerit, sicut Jesus mortuus est, quando voluit?
english: Who so falls asleep when he wills, as Jesus died when He willed?

latin: Quod ergo Rex esse voluit Israel, dignatio est non promotio, miserationis indicium, non potestatis augmentum; qui enim appellatus est in terra Rex Judæorum, in cœlis est Dominus Angelorum.
english: That he willed to be King of Israel is condescension, not promotion; a sign of his mercy, not an increase of his power; for he who on earth is called King of the Jews is in heaven the Lord of the Angels.

latin: Merito ab Oriente veniunt, qui Solem justitiæ novum nobis ortum annuntiant, lætisque rumoribus totum mundum illuminant.
english: Rightly do they come from the East, who proclaim to us the Sun of Justice newly risen, and with glad tidings fill the whole world with light.
from: The Star and the Three Kings

latin: Numquam enim Pater sine Filio fuit, numquam sine Verbo, numquam sine Virtute, numquam sine Sapientia esse potuit.
english: For never was the Father without the Son; never without the Word, never without his Power, never without his Wisdom could he be.
from: In the Beginning Was the Word

latin: Tantum enim desiderabant audire Christum, quod omiserant apportare secum alimentum; propter quod necessarium erat fieri miraculum.
english: So greatly did they long to hear Christ that they had forgotten to bring food with them; and for that reason a miracle had to be wrought.

latin: Ecce verbum miræ dulcedinis et amoris, procedens ex intimis cordis medullis, et pertingens suo vigore usque ad intima nostra.
english: Behold a word of wondrous sweetness and love, proceeding from the inmost marrow of the heart, and reaching by its power into our inmost depths.

latin: Et erit judicium illud horribile ex omni parte: supra, erit Judex iratus; subtus, patens infernus; intus conscientia remordens; extra, mundus ardens.
english: And that judgement will be horrible on every side: above, the angry Judge; below, hell lying open; within, conscience gnawing; without, the world burning.

latin: O quam beatam diem, et quam beatam noctem duxerunt, audientes et videntes eum: quem multi videre et audire volentes, non viderunt neque audierunt!
english: O how blessed a day, and how blessed a night, they passed, hearing and seeing Him: whom many, longing to see and to hear, neither saw nor heard!

latin: Ecce fugit Christus, et nocte fugit, ut fuga quæ per se difficilis est, noctis obscuritate difficilior fiat.
english: Behold, Christ flees, and He flees by night, so that the flight, which is hard in itself, is made harder still by the darkness of the night.

latin: Quæ generationes narrantur, propter illam qua Maria genuit Jesum, qui vocatur Christus.
english: These generations are recounted for the sake of that one by which Mary begot Jesus, who is called Christ.

latin: Non est voluntatis confessio, sed necessitatis extorsio, quia timent ejus præsentiam.
english: It is not the confession of the will, but the wresting of necessity, for they fear his presence.

latin: Unde hoc, Deus meus? Ita compateris mihi exhibens hominem, ut quodammodo videaris nescire quod Deus es?
english: Why this, my God? Do You so suffer with me, showing Yourself man, that in some way You seem not to know that You are God?

latin: Intrat per ostium, qui intrat per Christum, qui imitatur passionem Christi, qui cognoscit humilitatem Christi.
english: He enters by the door who enters through Christ — he who imitates the Passion of Christ, who knows the humility of Christ.

latin: Tunc est enim optimus homo, cum tota vita sua pergit in incommutabile bonum.
english: For a man is then at his best, when with his whole life he presses on toward the unchangeable Good.

latin: Quæritis me propter aliud; quærite me propter me, non propter aliud, qui sum cibus permanens in vitam æternam.
english: You seek Me for the sake of something else; seek Me for My own sake, not for anything else — I who am the food that endures unto life everlasting.
from: Seek Jesus for His Own Sake

latin: Propter quod Dominus Jesus cum hunc non solum oculo corporis, sed etiam miserationis vidisset, dicit ei: Vis sanus fieri?
english: And so the Lord Jesus, when He had seen this man not only with the eye of the body but with the eye of mercy, said to him: Wilt thou be made whole?

latin: Quem autem hostis mortuo similem reddidit, hunc Salvator piæ dexteræ tactu elevavit, in quo ostenditur Dei auxilium; et surrexit, in quo monstratur hominis studium.
english: But him whom the enemy had left like a corpse, the Saviour lifted up with the touch of His merciful right hand — in which is shown the help of God; and he arose — in which is shown the effort of man.

latin: Ecce quidam leprosus veniens, non tam passibus corporis, quam fide cordis, genu flexo, procidens in faciem suam, adorabat eum.
english: Behold, a certain leper came, not so much with the steps of his body as with the faith of his heart, and with bent knee, falling upon his face, he adored him.

latin: Jesus tamen ibidem remanens, solo jussu et verbo filium absens sanat, ut pater ejus amplius credat.
english: Yet Jesus, remaining in that very place, by command and word alone, being absent, heals the son — that his father might believe the more.
from: The Word That Heals from Afar

latin: Ego sum vermis et non homo, opprobrium hominum et abjectio plebis.
english: I am a worm and no man, the reproach of men and the outcast of the people.

latin: Illum oportet crescere, me autem minui.
english: He must increase, but I must decrease.
from: He Must Increase, I Must Decrease

latin: Obstupesce illam clarissimam dignationem, et dignatissimam caritatem, qua nobis tradidit et reliquit semetipsum, scilicet sacratissimum Corpus et Sanguinem suum vere in cibum et potum.
english: Be astonished at that most clear condescension, and that most worthy love, with which He handed over and left to us His very self, namely His most holy Body and Blood, truly for food and for drink.

latin: In isto foro, animæ hominum venales exponuntur. Mercatores duo sunt: Deus et diabolus.
english: In this marketplace the souls of men are set out for sale. There are two merchants: God and the devil.

latin: Unus panis quem secum habebant in navi, mystice ipsum panem vitæ Dominum, videlicet Salvatorem designat; cujus amore, quia semper intus reficiebantur in corde, minus de terreno pane curabant.
english: The one loaf they had with them in the boat mystically signifies the Lord Himself, the Bread of life, the Saviour; for the love of whom, because they were ever refreshed within in their heart, they cared the less for earthly bread.
from: The One Bread in the Boat

latin: Simplex enim aequitas, et indocta hominum rusticitas saepe veritatem rei sine fraude manifestat; at contra versuta malignitas falsitatem verisimilibus verbis pro vero commendare decertat.
english: Simple honesty and the unschooled plainness of men often make the truth of a thing known without deceit; but cunning malice, on the contrary, strains to commend falsehood as truth with plausible words.

latin: Petrus quidem moras navis exspectare non potuit, sed amoris immensitate cogente, in mare se praecipitavit, ut velocius et magis festinanter ad Dominum suum venire posset.
english: Peter could not wait for the slowness of the boat, but, driven by the immensity of love, he hurled himself into the sea, that he might come more swiftly and in greater haste to his Lord.

latin: Dubitatio quidem discipulorum, nostræ fidei fuit confirmatio: quippe quo amplius dubitaverunt, eo sollicitius inquisierunt, et quo verius probaverunt, eo firmius crediderunt.
english: The doubt of the disciples was indeed the confirmation of our faith: for the more they doubted, the more anxiously they inquired; and the more truly they proved, the more firmly they believed.

latin: Frater mi, comede panem tuum, quia Filius hominis a mortuis resurrexit.
english: Brother, eat your bread, for the Son of Man is risen from the dead.

latin: Stetit in medio discipulorum: sicut sol inter sidera, ut eos illuminaret; sicut lilium inter lilia, ut eos decoraret; sicut princeps inter milites, ut eos roboraret; sicut pater inter filios, ut eos concordaret.
english: He stood in the midst of the disciples: as the sun among the stars, to give them light; as the lily among lilies, to adorn them; as the prince among his soldiers, to make them strong; as a father among his sons, to bring them to one heart.

latin: Dominus Jesus discipulos consolando, fecit eis sermonem pulcherrimum, ignitis plenum carbonibus dulcedinis et amoris.
english: The Lord Jesus, consoling his disciples, made for them a most beautiful sermon, full of glowing coals of sweetness and of love.
from: Let Not Your Heart Be Troubled

latin: Fons enim pietatis erat, et ideo lacrymas continere non poterat, nec ædificia planxit parietum, sed subversionem animarum.
english: For he was the fountain of mercy, and therefore he could not hold back his tears; nor did he weep for the walls of the buildings, but for the overthrow of souls.

latin: Ipse vero aliter disposuerat, qui universorum salutem sitiebat.
english: But He had ordained otherwise, He who thirsted for the salvation of all.
from: The Lord Seated upon the Colt

latin: Et, secundum Ambrosium, humeri Christi crucis brachia sunt: illic mea peccata deposui, in illa patibuli nobilis cruce quievi.
english: And, according to Ambrose, the shoulders of Christ are the arms of the Cross: there I laid down my sins; upon that noble gibbet, that Cross, I found rest.
from: The Shepherd Who Carries Me Home

latin: Domine Jesu Christe, qui illuminasti oculos cæci nati, illumina, quæso, oculos cordis mei, ne in tenebris offendam, vel umquam in morte obdormiam.
english: Lord Jesus Christ, who gave light to the eyes of the man born blind, give light, I beseech you, to the eyes of my heart, that I may not stumble in the darkness, nor ever fall asleep in death.

latin: Tetigit eum, non causa necessitatis, sed humilitatis; et ut divinitatem in carne ostenderet, eum sanavit.
english: He touched him, not from necessity, but from humility; and that he might show his divinity in the flesh, he made him whole.

latin: Dico verbo, et sanatur infirmus; et Legem sabbati vestri non transgredior, et sine opere opus virtutis consummo.
english: I speak a word, and the sick man is healed; I do not break your law of the sabbath, and without labour I complete a work of power.

latin: Operarii sunt in Ecclesia qui quærunt opus, non otium; onus, non honorem; animas, non decimas; prodesse, non præesse.
english: The laborers in the Church are those who seek work, not idleness; the burden, not the honor; souls, not tithes; to be of profit, not to preside.

latin: Ad locum unde exeunt flumina revertuntur, ut iterum fluant.
english: Unto the place whence the rivers come forth, they return, that they may flow again.

latin: Quam angusta porta et arcta via est, quæ ducit ad vitam; et pauci sunt qui inveniunt eam!
english: How strait is the gate, and narrow is the way that leadeth unto life; and few there are that find it!

latin: Verbum æternum non nascitur, nisi in corde pacato; unde Psalmus dicit: Factus est in pace locus ejus.
english: The eternal Word is not born except in a heart at peace; whence the Psalm says: His place is made in peace.

latin: Nox enim quasi unus carpentator de dicta arbore tollit unum scopulum, et dies similiter alium; quot enim diebus et noctibus vixit homo, tot minus habet vivere, et ad ultimum necesse habet arbor cadere.
english: For night, like a woodcutter, takes one chip from the tree, and the day likewise takes another; for however many days and nights a man has lived, by so much less has he left to live, and at the last the tree must fall.

latin: O quanta fiducia et fiducialis audacia, quod factura Factorem, creatura Creatorem, homo Deum Patrem audeat nuncupare!
english: O what confidence, what daring trust, that the thing made should dare to name its Maker, the creature its Creator, a man should dare to call God his Father!

latin: Ignis gehennæ lucebit miseris ad miseriæ augmentum, ut videant unde doleant; non ad consolationem, ut videant unde gaudeant.
english: The fire of hell will shine upon the wretched only to increase their misery — that they may see whereby they grieve, not for any comfort, that they might see whereby they should rejoice.
from: The Pains of Hell and the Glory of Heaven

latin: Item, dicitur cœna magna, imo sic maxima, ut ejus immensitas in cor hominis non possit ascendere.
english: Again, it is called a great supper — nay, so great that its immensity cannot rise up into the heart of man.

latin: Pauca sunt quæ in hoc seculo nobis præstantur, multa quæ in illo servantur.
english: Few are the things bestowed on us in this world; many are the things kept for us in that one.

latin: Qui ubique præsens est, diligentibus præsentior, et negligentibus absentior est.
english: He who is everywhere present is more present to those who love him, and more absent to those who neglect him.
from: The Vineyard Let Out to Tenants

latin: Exiit autem qui ubique est, non localiter sed Incarnatione; et per amictum carnis propinquior nobis factus est.
english: He who is everywhere went out — not by changing place, but by the Incarnation; and through the garment of flesh he was made nearer to us.

latin: Magna virtus, ubi sine mora salus imperium comitatur.
english: Great is that power, where healing follows the command without any delay.

latin: Itaque in cruce Domini est finis Legis atque Scripturæ, in Passione ejus summa omnis perfectionis, in morte ipsius est consummatio omnis sermonis.
english: And so on the cross of the Lord is the end of the Law and of Scripture; in his Passion, the sum of all perfection; in his death, the consummation of every word.

latin: Nemo enim bonus, nisi solus et unus Deus.
english: For no one is good, except the one and only God.

latin: Temporalia perdere timuerunt, et æterna non cogitaverunt, et sic utraque amiserunt.
english: They feared to lose the things of time, and did not weigh the eternal; and so they lost them both.

latin: Ad laudandum igitur Conditorem tuum creata es, ut ejus laudibus vacans, et hic justitiæ merito in illum semper proficias, et in futuro beate vivas.
english: You were created, then, to praise your Maker, so that, giving yourself to His praises, you may here ever advance toward Him by the merit of righteousness, and hereafter live in blessedness.

latin: Christus Mariæ Filius est candela accensa, propter tria quæ inveniuntur in ea, scilicet lumen, ignis, et cera; et in Christo sunt tria, scilicet caro, anima et deitas vera.
english: Christ, the Son of Mary, is the lighted candle, because of the three things found in it — light, fire, and wax; and in Christ there are three — flesh, soul, and true Godhead.

latin: Non oportebat eum horrea dilatare, bene erant horrea parata, scilicet pauperum esurientium ventres.
english: He need not have enlarged his barns: good barns stood ready — the bellies of the hungry poor.

latin: Sed generatio Judaica non plorat cum Joanne plorante, neque gaudet cum Christo gaudente.
english: But the Jewish generation does not weep with John weeping, nor rejoice with Christ rejoicing.

latin: Inveniatur ergo qui ex charitate moriatur, non obnoxius morti; et sic mors non poterit tenere innoxium, sed faciet in ea foramen, per quod transeant liberati.
english: Let one be found, then, who dies out of love, not owing death; and so death cannot hold the innocent one, but He will make in it an opening, through which the freed may pass.

latin: Injuria nimia ubi falsi tradunt veritatem, fatui illudunt sapientiam, rei flagellant innocentiam, miseri conspuunt gloriam, mortui occidunt vitam.
english: Outrage beyond measure: where the false betray the Truth, fools mock Wisdom, the guilty scourge Innocence, the wretched spit upon Glory, the dead slay Life.
from: The Cup He Came to Drink

latin: Radix amara crucis evanuit, flos vitæ cum fructibus erupit; et qui jacuit in morte, surrexit in gloria; sol post occasum oritur.
english: The bitter root of the cross has faded; the flower of life has burst forth with its fruits; and he who lay in death has risen in glory; the sun rises after its setting.
from: The New Day After the Sabbath

latin: O quantum Puer egregius ac delicatus, Rex cæli et terræ, pro nobis laboravit, et quam cito cæperit!
english: O how greatly this noble and tender Child, the King of heaven and earth, labored for us, and how early he began!

latin: Nam ideo nudi accedimus ad lavacrum, ut nudi ad cœli januam properemus.
english: For we come naked to the font, that naked we may hasten to the gate of heaven.

latin: Divitibus sunt nomina in culmine mundi, sed pauperibus sunt nomina in aula Dei.
english: The rich have their names on the summit of the world, but the poor have their names in the hall of God.
from: The Rich Man and Lazarus at the Gate

latin: Sic quotidie facit nobiscum spiritualiter: si enim aliqua perplexitate vel accidia gravati pie de ipso loquimur, statim adest, confortans corda nostra et illuminans, et etiam ad amorem suum inflammans.
english: So he does with us each day in spirit: for if, weighed down by some perplexity or listlessness, we speak of him devoutly, at once he is present, strengthening our hearts and enlightening them, and setting them aflame with love of himself.

latin: Cum igitur tribulatio advenerit, exi obviam hospiti tanto, benigne eum recipiendo; et dic corde jucundo: Bene venerit amica mea, tribulatio.
english: When therefore tribulation comes, go out to meet so great a guest, receiving it kindly; and say with a glad heart: Welcome, my friend, tribulation.

latin: Non cathedra facit sacerdotem, sed sacerdos cathedram; non locus sanctificat hominem, sed homo locum.
english: The chair does not make the priest, but the priest the chair; the place does not sanctify the man, but the man the place.

latin: Ad transeundum istum mundum vidit Christus, id est approbavit duas naves.
english: To cross over this world, Christ saw — that is, approved — two boats.

latin: Sicut enim templum est in civitate, ita vita sacerdotum in plebe.
english: For as the temple stands in the city, so stands the life of the priests among the people.

latin: Vere enim nullum viderunt, quia corporali aspectu viderunt, non spirituali affectu.
english: For truly they saw nothing, because they saw with the bodily eye, but not with spiritual affection.

latin: Aufertur ergo umbra, et palmam tenet veritas per orbem declarata.
english: The shadow, then, is taken away, and truth, declared throughout the world, holds the palm of victory.

latin: Quasi enim homo navem ascendit, sed quasi Deus mare conturbat; quasi homo in navi dormit, sed quasi Deus ventis et mari imperat, et eorum furorem verbo coercet.
english: For as man He boards the boat, but as God He troubles the sea; as man He sleeps in the boat, but as God He commands the winds and the sea, and restrains their fury by a word.
from: The Master Asleep in the Boat

latin: Quid primum miror hic nescio, an præsentiam stateris in ore piscis, an magnitudinem virtutis, si ad verbum ejus mox stater in ore piscis creatus est!
english: What I marvel at first here I do not know — whether the presence of the stater in the fish's mouth, or the greatness of the power, if at His word a stater was at that moment created in the fish's mouth!
from: The Coin in the Fish's Mouth

latin: Sic ergo abstiterunt loco, sed facti sunt proximi deprecando.
english: And so they stood apart in place, but they were made near by their pleading.

latin: O quantus clamor, quia non erit anima ita alte in cœlo, nec ita profunde in inferno, nec corpus in sepulchro, quin audiat hunc clamorem, et contremiscat!
english: O how great that cry shall be! For no soul will be so high in heaven, nor so deep in hell, nor any body in the grave, but it will hear this cry, and tremble!

latin: Necessarium est eos qui panem de cœlo descendentem participare desiderant, crebro eleemosynis, lacrymis, et aliis justitiæ fructibus sua opera purgare.
english: It is needful that those who desire to share the Bread that comes down from heaven should cleanse their works again and again — with alms, with tears, and with the other fruits of righteousness.

latin: Resplenduit enim facies ejus sicut sol, imo plus quam sol, sed nihil habet clarius cui comparetur.
english: For His face shone as the sun — no, more than the sun, yet there is nothing brighter to which He might be compared.

latin: Sicut Cæsar exigit impressionem suæ imaginis, sic et Deus animam lumine vultus sui insignitam.
english: As Caesar demands the stamp of his own image, so also God demands the soul marked with the light of his countenance.

latin: Sic est in terra animæ, quia tota fortitudo ejus consistit in ingressu, scilicet in occasionibus peccatorum, et propter hoc necessaria est bona custodia circa ipsum.
english: So it is in the land of the soul: for its whole strength stands in the entrance — that is, in the occasions of sins — and for this a good watch about it is necessary.

latin: Felix tactus, ad quem tanta sequitur virtus!
english: O happy touch, upon which so great a power follows!

latin: Sicut enim rosa antequam videatur sentitur, et antequam inveniatur tenetur in odoribus suis; sic et Domino transeunte, de longe duo cæci, antequam eum viderent, divinitatem ejus fragrantia senserunt.
english: For just as a rose is sensed before it is seen, and before it is found is held fast in its own fragrance; so too, as the Lord passed by, the two blind men from afar, before they saw Him, perceived His divinity by its fragrance.
from: The Two Blind Men Beyond Jericho

latin: Tam validus denique es ad justificandum, quam multus ad ignoscendum.
english: So mighty art Thou, at the last, to justify, as Thou art abundant to forgive.

latin: O verbum strictum! O verbum anxium! O verbum durum discussionis districtæ tunc faciendæ, quando exigetur a nobis ratio de occultis cordium cogitationibus, de otiosis sermonibus, et de nostris operibus!
english: O strict word! O anxious word! O hard word of the searching trial then to be made, when the reckoning shall be demanded of us for the hidden thoughts of the heart, for idle words, and for our deeds!

latin: Horam vero ultimam Dominus noster idcirco nobis voluit esse incognitam, ut possit semper esse suspecta, ut dum illam praevidere minime possumus, ad illam sine intermissione praeparemur.
english: Our Lord willed the last hour to be unknown to us for this reason: that it might always be suspected, so that, while we are least able to foresee it, we might prepare for it without ceasing.

latin: O miserrimus mundus, et miseri qui eum sequuntur! Semper enim mundialia opera homines excluserunt a vita.
english: O most wretched world, and wretched they who follow it! For worldly works have always shut men out from life.

latin: Non quantum, sed ex quanto dederis, considerat Deus.
english: God considers not how much, but out of how much, you give.

latin: De die sunt simul in pascuis oves et hædi, sed in vespere pastor eos segregat.
english: By day the sheep and the goats are together in the pastures, but in the evening the shepherd separates them.

latin: Simulata æquitas non est æquitas, sed duplex iniquitas.
english: Feigned righteousness is not righteousness, but a double iniquity.

latin: Venit enim non perdere quod invenerit, sed quærere quod perierat.
english: For he came not to destroy what he had found, but to seek what had perished.

latin: Si tetigero tantum vestimentum ejus, salva ab infirmitate mea ero.
english: If I shall touch only His garment, I shall be healed of my infirmity.
from: If I Only Touch the Hem

latin: Felix Maria, id est anima, quæ ad sepulchrum Christi venit videre, per meditationem, plangere per compassionem, ac ungere per devotionem!
english: Happy the Mary — that is, the soul — who comes to the tomb of Christ: to see Him through meditation, to mourn Him through compassion, and to anoint Him through devotion!

latin: Lux mundi a Patre egrediens nube carnis tegitur; et sic contemperata toleranda hominibus efficitur, ut per hominem veniatur ad divinitatem.
english: The light of the world, going forth from the Father, is veiled in the cloud of flesh; and so tempered, it becomes bearable to men, that through the man we may come to the Godhead.

latin: Humiles quidem in seculo, etiam in ipso artis officio, sed præcelsos fide, ac devotos mentis obsequio; ignobiles seculo, sed nobiles Christo; despectos terræ, sed acceptissimos cælo; pauperes mundo, sed divites Deo.
english: Humble indeed in the world, even in their very trade, yet lofty in faith and devoted in the service of mind; lowborn to the world, but noble to Christ; despised of earth, but most welcome to heaven; poor to the world, but rich to God.
from: The Zeal of Christ in Preaching

latin: Amor est animæ pondus portans eam quocumque ipse fertur; et anima verius est ubi amat, quam ubi animat.
english: Love is the weight of the soul, carrying it wherever it is borne; and the soul is more truly where it loves than where it gives life.

latin: Ex quo universum conculcat tumorem, dum is qui exit a Deo, et ad Deum vadit, pedes lavare voluit.
english: By this he treads down all swelling pride, when he who comes from God and goes to God willed to wash men's feet.

latin: Oratio humilitatis se nubes penetrabit, et donec appropinquet non consolabitur; et non discedet donec aspiciat Altissimus.
english: The prayer of one who humbles himself shall pierce the clouds, and it will not be consoled until it draws near; and it will not depart until the Most High looks upon it.
from: Watching and Praying for the Coming

latin: Sciens quod amanti sufficit solum insinuare necessitatem, absque ulla petitione.
english: Knowing that, for one who loves, it is enough only to hint at the need, without any petition at all.

latin: Domine Jesu Christe, veni in Naim, animam meam fluctuantem tentationibus.
english: Lord Jesus Christ, come into Nain, into my soul tossed by temptations.

latin: Ecce sycomorum; ascende et tu lignum ubi pro te pependit Jesus, et videbis Jesum.
english: Behold the sycamore: climb, you also, the wood where Jesus hung for you, and you shall see Jesus.
